questions.q: Golconda was the seat of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Golconda was the seat of the Qutb Shahi dynasty of the Deccan.

questions.q: Golconda is best described as which kind of place?
questions.explanation: Golconda was a fortified citadel and the capital of the Qutb Shahi kingdom.

questions.q: Golconda is famous for trade in which precious commodity?
questions.explanation: Golconda was famed as a centre of the diamond trade.

questions.q: Golconda fort lies near which modern Indian city?
questions.explanation: The fort of Golconda is located near the city of Hyderabad.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor finally captured Golconda in 1687?
questions.explanation: The Mughal emperor Aurangzeb captured Golconda in 1687.

questions.q: Golconda fort is renowned for what unusual architectural feature?
questions.explanation: Golconda is famous for its remarkable acoustics, by which sounds carried across the fort.

questions.q: In which region of India is Golconda situated?
questions.explanation: Golconda lies on the Deccan plateau in southern India.

questions.q: From roughly which century did Golconda serve as the Qutb Shahi capital?
questions.explanation: Golconda served as the seat of the Qutb Shahi dynasty from the 16th century.

questions.q: Golconda was one of which group of post-Bahmani states?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahi kingdom of Golconda was one of the five Deccan sultanates.

questions.q: Golconda's fall to the Mughals in 1687 occurred during whose long campaigns in the Deccan?
questions.explanation: Golconda fell during Aurangzeb's protracted Deccan campaigns of the late 17th century.

questions.q: Who founded the Qutb Shahi dynasty that ruled from Golconda?
questions.explanation: Sultan Quli Qutb-ul-Mulk founded the Qutb Shahi dynasty at Golconda in the early 16th century.

questions.q: Which nearby planned city was founded by a Qutb Shahi ruler as Golconda grew crowded?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahis founded Hyderabad nearby, easing congestion in the older fort of Golconda.

questions.q: Which famous monument in Hyderabad was built by the Qutb Shahi ruler Muhammad Quli Qutb Shah?
questions.explanation: The Charminar was built in Hyderabad by Muhammad Quli Qutb Shah, founder of the city.

questions.q: The Kollur mine, source of legendary diamonds traded at Golconda, lay near which river?
questions.explanation: The Kollur diamond mine lay along the Krishna river, feeding Golconda's diamond markets.

questions.q: Which legendary diamond is traditionally associated with the Golconda region's mines?
questions.explanation: The Koh-i-Noor and other famous diamonds are traditionally linked to the Golconda mines.

questions.q: Golconda emerged as an independent sultanate after the breakup of which earlier Deccan state?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahi state of Golconda arose from the fragmentation of the Bahmani Sultanate.

questions.q: Before the Qutb Shahis, Golconda had been a stronghold of which earlier Telugu dynasty?
questions.explanation: Golconda was originally a hill fort of the Kakatiya dynasty before the Qutb Shahis rebuilt it.

questions.q: How is the acoustic feature of Golconda's gateway traditionally said to have functioned?
options: Bells rang on their own at sunrise
A clap at the entrance could be heard at the hilltop pavilion
Whispers were silenced by the walls
questions.explanation: A hand-clap near the main gate could reportedly be heard at the distant hilltop pavilion, serving as a warning system.

questions.q: The royal tombs of the Qutb Shahi rulers stand close to which fort?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahi tombs lie just outside the Golconda fort complex.

questions.q: Golconda was a member of the Deccan alliance that defeated Vijayanagara at which battle?
questions.explanation: Golconda joined the Deccan alliance that crushed Vijayanagara at Talikota in 1565.

questions.q: Which European trading powers were active buyers in Golconda's diamond markets in the 17th century?
questions.explanation: English, Dutch and French traders all sought the diamonds of the Golconda region.

questions.q: Which French jewel merchant left famous accounts of the diamond trade around Golconda?
questions.explanation: Jean-Baptiste Tavernier, a French jeweller-traveller, described the Golconda diamond mines and trade.

questions.q: Who was the last Qutb Shahi sultan of Golconda, defeated by Aurangzeb in 1687?
questions.explanation: Abul Hasan Qutb Shah, called Tana Shah, was the last ruler of Golconda before its Mughal conquest.

questions.q: How is the Mughal capture of Golconda in 1687 generally said to have been achieved?
options: By a single frontal assault in a day
By treachery from within after a long siege
questions.explanation: After a long siege, Golconda fell when a defender reportedly opened a gate, allowing the Mughals in.

questions.q: Roughly how many years did the Qutb Shahi dynasty rule from Golconda and Hyderabad before 1687?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahi dynasty ruled for roughly 170 years from its founding in 1518 to its fall in 1687.

questions.q: Which celebrated minister of Golconda was a noted patron and statesman under Abul Hasan?
questions.explanation: The Brahmin brothers Madanna and Akkanna served as influential ministers in the late Golconda court.

questions.q: Golconda's powerful general Mir Jumla defected to which power before the kingdom's final fall?
questions.explanation: Mir Jumla, a leading Golconda commander and diamond magnate, defected to the Mughals, weakening the kingdom.

questions.q: The Qutb Shahi rulers were notable patrons of which literary language of the Deccan?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahi court patronised Dakhni Urdu, and several sultans were themselves poets.

questions.q: Before Aurangzeb's final conquest, which Mughal emperor's forces had earlier forced Golconda into tributary submission?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's forces compelled Golconda to accept Mughal suzerainty and pay tribute decades before 1687.

questions.q: After 1687, the Golconda-Hyderabad region eventually came under the rule of which 18th-century dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Asaf Jahi Nizams later established their state at Hyderabad, the former Qutb Shahi heartland.
